items.caption_text:
Hi friends! Happy super full moon in taurus — hope you’re slowing down, reevaluating and getting some earth beneath your feet

Not sure who will even see this (less than 1% of you apparently;) and honestly, I’m not sure what’s to become of this space anymore. It’s not about the numbers, but wow, do I miss what it used to be. The connection, the conversations, the way it felt like an actual community instead of just 'content' and 'reels'

Even if this app fades away (or the algorithm keeps doing whatever it's doing), I’ll still be...somewhere...sharing all the 'living better with less' ways that keep me rooted. Slowing down, simplifying, savoring the small things. Maybe on my personal account, or substack, maybe youtube, maybe just out in the real world with whoever’s around … or yelling it from a mountain top, who knows!

To those who’ve been here since the early days (hi 2010 crew!), thank you for making this place SO special for so long.

This isn’t goodbye, far from it. Just a shift

Let’s take things offline a bit more, host, create, mend, consume less, use what we have, share resources, keep finding each other — wherever that may be. 🌿

items.caption_text:
Can we stop pretending that buying more stuff is the answer;) I used to think so, until I realized my wardrobe needed less stuff and more style. These days, it’s all about slow, intentional shopping—secondhand treasures, repairing and reworking what I already have, and choosing pieces that actually reflect who I am. No trends, no impulse buys, just a closet that feels like me. Want to know how I got here? Head to my latest post where I dive into how I built a wardrobe I actually love. 

livingbetterwithless.substack.com

items.caption_text:
Fresh blanket of snow = time for a wool refresh

How To:
• lay your knits on fresh, clean snow
• lightly cover with snow
• very gently rub snow into the wool (the ice crystals scrub the fibers without damaging or felting)
• flip over and repeat
• let the sweaters lay in the snow for 15-20 minutes (the cold really helps kill any built up smell/stink in the fibers)
• gently shake sweaters out, reshape and lay flat to dry

#woolsweater #woolrefresh #naturalfibers #woolcare

items.caption_text:
Whenever I get the urge to shop for new clothes or feel uninspired by my wardrobe—a simple button-swap on a  sweater will often do the trick. It brings new life to a forgotten piece and makes me fall in love with it all over again! 🪡✨ 

#buynothingnew #usewhatyouhave #repairdontreplace #mendingmatters #diy

items.caption_text:
Consumption rates vary widely by region, but overall, we're using the planet’s resources 1.7 times faster than they can regenerate, according to @guardian. This means we'd need 1.7 Earths to maintain our current lifestyle, which is clearly unsustainable. While population growth plays a role, we’re also consuming more as individuals than ever before.

I’d love to hear your thoughts and how you’re making small changes to reduce your impact!

items.caption_text:
@lightprovisions has done it y'all—they've created a truly package-free candle refill system. This is a game changer! After years of trials and testing, Brett of Light Provisions has unlocked the perfect candle refill solution.

No more cluttered cupboards and too many candle vessels you don't know what to do with. I mean, you can only repurpose so many, right!?

Simply secure your refill pillar* to the base of your favorite container* or previously loved LP crystal tumbler. Then watch as the candle beautifully collapses, filling your chosen vessel with all that liquid gold—ahem wax. Before you know it, it's burning like a brand new candle; without all the extra packaging. It's brilliance and beauty all wrapped into one. 

Right now, they're offering two scents: Palo Santo & Ginger and Unscented. More scents in the works, so please support this ingenious and sustainably executed refill program so they can launch more of these good smells! 

(If you're new around here: 99.9% of candles give me painful headaches. Because Light Provisions uses soy and other natural plant materials, these are the only candles that I burn – they're pure perfection!)

*Note: these are not stand alone candle pillars/votives. They're designed to collapse, so make sure you follow their burning and container directions upon purchase;)

items.caption_text: “But what can I do, I am just one person" ... We are not powerless. Each one of us holds the potential to contribute to change. Our collective voices can be a powerful force for relief and transformation. Let's challenge the sense of helplessness and unite our voices, petitions, and power to fortify our hearts. Every effort counts in our unwavering pursuit of justice and liberation. ... "said 7 billion people"

items.caption_text:
Finally tackling my sky-high mending pile. Instead of trying to hide the repairs I'm opting for visible thread and decorative techniques — it's a work in progress to say the least.

Sharing more in today's newsletter. Find it in your inbox or follow the link in bio to read & subscribe 〰️

#sashiko #boro #visiblemending #repairdontreplace #handstitched #sashikostitching #wabisabi #sustainablefashion #jeansrepair

items.caption_text:
Oldie but a goodie from Pete Seeger. 

If it can't be reduced -
If it can't be reduced
Reused, repaired - REUSED REPAIRED
Rebuilt, refurbished, refinished, resold
Recycled or composted - OR COMPOSTED
Then it should be - THEN IT SHOULD BE
Restricted, redesigned - RESTRICTED
REDESIGNED or removed - REMOVED!
From production - FROM PRODUCTION

items.caption_text: It's seed saving season! 🌻🌸🌼 Deadheading / collecting all the Cosmos, Zinnias, Sunflowers, Hollyhocks, Marigolds, Cornflowers, Larkspur, and so many more(!) from the garden that I can. Once properly dried, the seeds will be stored in this airtight container until spring! (Ahem, pollinator meadow;)
